steps.title: Summarize what the narrator actually does in the passage
steps.content: Sugimoto says the standards of her two countries — Japan and the US — differ widely, and that she loves both. She pictures herself floating above and looking down at both worlds with "measuring eyes," and she keeps trying to interpret unfamiliar American customs by Japanese standards.

steps.title: Translate that into a main purpose
steps.content: The text is doing one thing: capturing what it feels like to observe two cultures she cares about and to actively try to make sense of the differences between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'observing and making sense of differences between two cultures she embraces': matches both the "measuring eyes" observing and the active attempt to explain unfamiliar customs, and matches her sincere love for both lands. ✓
  • Choice B — 'hope of forming connections... by sharing customs she learned as a child': overreach — the passage never mentions companions or sharing her own customs; she is interpreting, not teaching. ✗
  • Choice C — 'hesitant to ask questions': misread — the text says no one around her seemed to know the origins of customs, not that she was reluctant to ask. ✗
  • Choice D — 'wonder at... physical distance between two countries': misread — the "cloud in space" image is a metaphor for cultural perspective, not a literal claim about geographic distance. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
